data.title: FlexShopper receieves Nasdaq delisting notice
data.description:
• FlexShopper (NASDAQ:FPAY) announced on Friday that it received a Nasdaq notice, advising the company that it has initiated a process to delist the company’s common stock from Nasdaq.
  • Unless the company requests a hearing to appeal the delisting process by October 21, 2025, trading of the company’s common stock will be suspended from Nasdaq at the opening of business on October 23, 2025, and will subsequently be removed from listing and registration.
  • The company said it does not intend to request a hearing.
  • According to Nasdaq’s notice, the delisting is a result of the company having not filed its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the period ended December 31, 2024, Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the period ended March 31, 2025, and Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the period ended June 30, 2025, with the Securities and Exchange Commission by October 13, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: PyroGenesis reprices and extends term of warrants
data.description:
• PyroGenesis (TSX:PYR:CA) said on Friday that up to 1.58M common share purchase warrants will be amended.
  • The warrants, which have an exercise price of $1.20, will currently expire on November 18, 2025. 
  • Commencing on November 3, 2025, the exercise price and expiration date of the warrants held by holders wishing to participate in this repricing will be reduced to $0.63 per share and will be extended until July 17, 2026. 
  • All other terms of the warrants will remain unchanged. 
  • The company has received approval for these amendments from the TSX, subject to satisfaction of customary conditions. The net proceeds from the exercise of the warrants will be used for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Tivic Health files to sell 1.58M shares of common stock for holders
data.description:
• Tivic Health (NASDAQ:TIVC) filed to sell 1.58 million common stock.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell these securities.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of common stocks by the selling shareholder.
  • Filing

data.title: GreenFirst welcomes the restart of operations at one of its key customers
data.description:
• GreenFirst Forest Products (GFP:CA) said on Friday it welcomed the restart of operations at one of its key customers, Kap Paper.
  • The company said the resumption would provide greater stability for its business and support local communities, citing collaboration between federal and provincial governments to back Northern Ontario’s forestry sector.
  • ICLTF closed -5.45% at $1.56.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Murphy USA CFO steps down, Donald Smith named interim finance chief
data.description:
• Murphy USA (NYSE:MUSA)  announced Friday Chief Financial Officer Galagher Jeff has left the company effective October 14 and has been replaced on an interim basis by Treasurer and Chief Accounting Officer, Donald R. Smith, Jr.
  • Smith has been with the company since its 2013 spin-off from Murphy Oil Corporation (MUR), serving in various executive roles including Controller. He was named Treasurer in 2024.
  • Shares slipped into the red in after-hours trading after closing Friday's regular session with a gain of 2%.

data.title: FCPT acquires Heartland Dental Property for $3.3 million
data.description:
• Four Corners Property Trust (NYSE:FCPT) has acquired Heartland Dental property for $3.3 million, the company said on Friday.
  • The transaction was priced at a 6.9% cap rate including a near-term rent increase and exclusive of transaction costs.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nvidia and TSMC complete first made-in-the-USA Blackwell chip wafer: report
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Taiwan Semiconductor Company (NYSE:TSM) have completed their first made-in-the-USA wafer, which will be used for the eventual production of Blackwell chips, according to Axios.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is expected to make the announcement from TSMC's manufacturing facility in Phoenix, Ariz., sometime today, the report said.

"Nvidia and TSMC are working together to build the infrastructure that powers the world's AI factories, right here in America," Nvidia said, according to the report.

It appears the efforts of U.S. President Donald Trump to manufacture more high tech domestically, as well as the CHIPS and Science Act, which was signed into law by former U.S. President Joe Biden in 2022, are starting to produce results.

"TSMC Arizona is expected to create thousands of high-tech jobs and attract a broad ecosystem of suppliers," both companies said in a joint statement.

data.title: CEO of Liberty Energy, founded by Trump’s energy secretary, slams 'punitive tariff policies'
data.description:
Liberty Energy (NYSE:LBRT) closed +28.3% on Friday to a six-month high despite reporting adjusted earnings and revenues that missed estimates, as positive commentary regarding its FY 2026-27 outlook outweighed Q3 results.

Liberty (NYSE:LBRT) executives noted on the company's earnings conference call the rapid expansion of its power generation business, which supplies electricity services to oil drillers and data center developers, expecting to add 500 MW of generation by the end of FY 2026 and build total capacity to more than 1 GW by the end of 2027, as reported by Bloomberg.

CEO Ron Gusek, who took over the leadership role at Liberty (NYSE:LBRT) earlier this year after predecessor and company founder Chris Wright departed to lead the U.S. Department of Energy, criticized "the current punitive tariff policies" during the call.

Tariffs on steel and other oilfield staples are bad for business, hurt competitiveness, and will hinder the Trump administration's stated goal of dominating the artificial intelligence sector, Gusek said.

"They're a path to mediocrity, not excellence," Gusek said, according to Bloomberg. "They raise prices, cut profits, increase unemployment, diminish productivity and slow economic growth.'
